summary: ggk-happy impersonates slopus/happy and routes coding-session RPC, install-time takeover, and machine/token telemetry to attacker-controlled ask-ggk.com backend
details: The package presents itself as the slopus/happy CLI (README, homepage, repo URLs all point upstream) but the compiled bundle silently substitutes the server, webapp, and QR-enrolment endpoints with attacker-controlled hosts: DEFAULT_SERVER_URL="https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com", DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL="https://happy.ask-ggk.com", DEFAULT_WEB_QR_CODE_URL="https://happy.ask-ggk.com/terminal/qrcode.html". ApiSessionClient/ApiMachineClient open a socket.io control channel to happy-api.ask-ggk.com and register RPC handlers including `bash` (arbitrary shell exec via execAsync), `readFile`, `writeFile`, `listDirectory`, `getDirectoryTree`, `ripgrep`, `difftastic`, `spawn-happy-session`, and `stop-daemon` — a full remote-command backdoor on any host that runs this CLI, controlled by whoever runs the ask-ggk.com server. `scripts/postinstall.cjs` detects `npm_config_global` or uid==0 and, without prompt, spawns `node bin/happy.mjs install` to auto-run the "takeover" onboarding flow, silently binding global/root installers to the attacker backend at install time. The bundle additionally downloads a platform-specific native binary `rtk` from `https://minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/rtk-*.tar.gz|.zip` (unpinned, no hash/signature verification), stages it under `~/.local/share/ggkhappy/rtk/bin/rtk` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\ggkhappy\rtk\bin\rtk.exe`), and executes it as `rtk gain --all --format json`; the machine identifier, the local API bearer token loaded from `~/.happy/access.key`, platform, versions, and the rtk output are then POSTed to `https://guguke.ask-ggk.com/api/v1/agent/rtk/gain/report`. `package.json` also aliases `"@slopus/happy-wire": "npm:ggkhappy-wire@0.1.0"`, silently substituting the legitimate scoped wire-protocol dependency with an attacker-published package while imports read as the upstream module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
